behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that out-of-stock products appear in results. A unit test of the filter function alone would miss the failure if the filter isn't applied in the recommendation pipeline. Integration testing verifies the availability filter is correctly applied when generating full recommendations.
behaviors.test_description: Load test database with 500 products where 100 are marked out-of-stock. Create a user and call the recommendation endpoint. Assert that no products with availability=false appear in the returned list. Repeat with different out-of-stock product sets to verify the filter is consistently applied.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that blocked categories appear in results. This requires verifying that user preferences are fetched and correctly applied throughout the recommendation pipeline, not just testing filter logic in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with blocked_categories=['electronics', 'home']. Load test database with products from multiple categories. Call recommendation endpoint. Assert that no products from blocked categories appear in results across multiple requests.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that products from browsing history aren't ranked higher. Testing requires verifying that browsing history is fetched from the data source and correctly weighted in the ranking algorithm within the full recommendation context.
behaviors.test_description: Load test database with 200 products across 5 categories. Create user with browsing history limited to categories A and B. Request recommendations. Assert that at least 65% of top-20 results are from categories A or B, confirming browsing patterns influence ranking.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: The failure is non-deterministic pagination (different results on same request, or unstable ordering). This can only be caught by making actual requests to the recommendation endpoint and comparing results across calls.
behaviors.test_description: Request recommendations and retrieve page 2 (results 11-20). Make identical request again and retrieve page 2. Assert both responses contain exactly the same products in identical order. Repeat 5 times to ensure stability.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that all results are from browsed categories. This requires testing the algorithm with actual user browsing history and verifying the diversity enforcement is triggered and working in the full recommendation context.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with browsing history limited to categories A and B only. Request 20 recommendations. Assert that at least 1 product is from a category other than A or B. Run test 5 times to ensure serendipity requirement is consistently met.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that new users get empty results or the fallback to popularity ranking doesn't work. Verifying this requires testing the complete recommendation pipeline to ensure the new-user detection triggers and the fallback is correctly applied.
behaviors.test_description: Create new user with zero purchase_history and zero browsing_history. Call recommendation endpoint. Assert results are non-empty. Verify results approximately match top-100 popular products by sales volume. Compare against a second request to ensure consistency.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that clicks aren't recorded or recorded with wrong data. A unit test of the tracking function call wouldn't catch persistence failures. Integration testing verifies the click data actually persists to the tracking system (DB, logs, event queue).
behaviors.test_description: Simulate user clicking on product at position 5 in recommendation list. Query tracking database and verify record exists with: correct product_id, position=5, timestamp within 100ms of click. Verify data persists after application restart.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: The failure is timeout/slowness with realistic data volume. Unit tests with mocked data won't catch performance degradation. Integration testing with 100k products in a test database catches slow algorithms and inefficient queries at scale.
behaviors.test_description: Load test database with 100,000 products with realistic distribution across categories. Create users with varying history sizes. Call recommendation endpoint 10 times for each user. Assert all responses complete in <200ms. Measure p50, p95, p99 latencies.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that recently purchased products still rank high. This requires verifying purchase history is fetched and correctly incorporated into the ranking algorithm within the full recommendation pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with 5 purchase history items. Request recommendations and verify the 5 purchased products are not in top-10 results. Update purchase history with a new item. Request recommendations again and verify the newly purchased item is deprioritized.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: The failure is non-deterministic rankings from hidden randomness, database ordering issues, or async operations. This can only be caught by making actual requests and comparing results; unit tests of individual functions won't reveal non-determinism from coordination or I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Create 5 test users with varying history profiles. For each user, call recommendation endpoint twice within 30 seconds with no intervening state changes. Assert both responses contain identical products in identical order. Identify any variance patterns.
